docs.text: wring A tool to compress multiple CSV data files into parquet Wring is available as wring on PyPI: pip install wring Wring is primarily a command line tool. wring . For an overview of options, see Usage .
docs.title: Home

docs.text: app Wring helps keep files small. Usage: wring [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]... Options: --install-completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell|pwsh] Install completion for the specified shell. --show-completion [bash|zsh|fish|powershell|pwsh] Show completion for the specified shell, to copy it or customize the installation. csv Crawl a directory and compress csv files into parquet. Usage: wring csv [OPTIONS] DIRECTORY Options: DIRECTORY [required] --compression TEXT compression algorithm [default: zstd] --compression-level INTEGER compression level [default: 9] --clean / --no-clean remove uncompressed files after successful wringing [default: no-clean] tarzst Compress a directory using the .tar.zst format. Usage: wring tarzst [OPTIONS] DIRECTORY Options: DIRECTORY the directory to compress [required] --archive PATH base file name for the resulting archive file(s), if not provided a '.tar.zst' suffix is added to the original directory name -c, --chunk-gigs FLOAT chunk the resulting archive into parts that are no larger than this many gigabytes untarzst Decompress a directory stored in .tar.zst format. Usage: wring untarzst [OPTIONS] ARCHIVE Options: ARCHIVE the archive file to decompress, or the first of multiple parts [required] --outdir PATH directory name for the resulting decompressed file(s), if not provided any '.tar.zst' suffix is stripped from the archive file name and the rest is used
docs.title: Usage

docs.text: Crawl a directory and compress csv files into parquet. Usage: wring csv [OPTIONS] DIRECTORY Options: DIRECTORY [required] --compression TEXT compression algorithm [default: zstd] --compression-level INTEGER compression level [default: 9] --clean / --no-clean remove uncompressed files after successful wringing [default: no-clean]
docs.title: csv

docs.text: Compress a directory using the .tar.zst format. Usage: wring tarzst [OPTIONS] DIRECTORY Options: DIRECTORY the directory to compress [required] --archive PATH base file name for the resulting archive file(s), if not provided a '.tar.zst' suffix is added to the original directory name -c, --chunk-gigs FLOAT chunk the resulting archive into parts that are no larger than this many gigabytes
docs.title: tarzst

docs.text: Decompress a directory stored in .tar.zst format. Usage: wring untarzst [OPTIONS] ARCHIVE Options: ARCHIVE the archive file to decompress, or the first of multiple parts [required] --outdir PATH directory name for the resulting decompressed file(s), if not provided any '.tar.zst' suffix is stripped from the archive file name and the rest is used
docs.title: untarzst
